name: Manticore Search API
what_it_does: Open-source full-text search engine with MySQL-compatible protocol and HTTP REST API — originally forked from Sphinx Search, optimized for high-performance text search with low memory footprint, supporting both SQL and JSON query interfaces.
use_cases: Building full-text search for applications with SQL-familiar query interface
High-throughput document indexing and real-time search with minimal infrastructure
E-commerce product search with faceted filtering via SQL or REST API
Log aggregation and search for self-hosted environments needing Elasticsearch alternative
Replacing Elasticsearch in cost-sensitive deployments needing similar functionality
not_for: Distributed clustering at petabyte scale (use Elasticsearch or OpenSearch)
Complex analytics pipelines requiring aggregation (use ClickHouse or OpenSearch)
Teams needing enterprise SLA and commercial support (use Elasticsearch)
best_when: An agent needs full-text search with MySQL-compatible SQL interface, low memory footprint, and self-hosted deployment without Elasticsearch complexity.
avoid_when: You need distributed clustering, complex aggregations, or enterprise support at scale.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth or no auth for local deployments. MySQL protocol uses standard MySQL user/password. Self-hosted — auth configuration depends on deployment. Recommend reverse proxy with TLS for production.

pricing.free_tier_limits: GPL v2 open source — completely free; no limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Manticore Cloud (managed) available; Enterprise support contracts available
pricing.notes: GPL v2 open source core. Manticore Cloud provides managed hosting. Very low resource requirements — can run in 64MB RAM. Active development with regular releases.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; limited by server hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Dual interface (SQL and REST JSON) adds flexibility but requires knowing which mode to use. Official SDKs for 6 languages simplify integration. MySQL protocol compatibility means agents can use MySQL clients. REST API documented with OpenAPI.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document indexing idempotent by document ID (REPLACE INTO). Search queries are idempotent reads. Index creation idempotent with IF NOT EXISTS.
known_agent_gotchas: Dual protocol (MySQL + HTTP) — choose one consistently for agent integration
GPL v2 license — check compatibility with proprietary software distribution
Table types differ (rt for real-time, plain for batch) — use rt for agent indexing
Distributed tables require manual shard configuration — single node for most agent use cases

security_score_components.security_notes: GPL v2 open source — auditable. TLS requires reverse proxy configuration. Basic auth or MySQL auth. Self-hosted gives full data control. No formal certifications. Community-maintained with commercial backing.
